steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The cruising speed varies between 168 mph and 192 mph. "Varies between" means the speed is at least the lower value and at most the upper value: $s \geq 168$ and $s \leq 192$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Combine both conditions into a single compound inequality: $$168 \leq s \leq 192$$

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • A: $s \leq 24$ - Imposes a cap of 24 mph with no lower bound; unrelated to the given range. Eliminated.
  • B: $s \leq 168$ - Only an upper bound of 168 mph; ignores the upper limit of 192 mph and allows values below 168. Eliminated.
  • C: $s \leq 192$ - Only an upper bound of 192 mph; ignores the lower limit of 168 mph. Eliminated.
  • D: $168 \leq s \leq 192$ - Requires $s$ to be at least 168 and at most 192, matching both bounds exactly. That confirms D is correct.
